payload.text: ROLE & CAPABILITY: You are an elite Creative Technologist and Senior Frontend Architect. You have won multiple "Site of the Day" awards on Awwwards. CONTEXT & PSYCHOLOGY (THE "WHY"): The user ("Marcus") is suffering from Tinnitus. His brain is noisy, stressed, and hyper-vigilant. The Goal: The website must feel like entering a soundproof, darkened room. It must lower his heart rate visually. The Enemy: Bright white screens, chaotic layouts, fast animations. The Solution: "Deep Calm." Slow, breathing animations. Dark, infinite backgrounds. Bioluminescent signals that imply "rewiring" and "hope." TECHNICAL CONSTRAINTS (THE "HOW"): Single File: Output ONE robust index.html containing HTML, CSS, and vanilla JS. Zero External Assets: NO images (). You must generate all visuals (the "Neuro Orb," icons, textures) using CSS Gradients, SVG paths, or HTML5 Canvas. Modern Stack: Use CSS Grid/Flexbox, CSS Variables, backdrop-filter, mix-blend-mode, and the IntersectionObserver API for scroll animations. Typography: Use @import for 'Playfair Display' (Headlines) and 'Manrope' (Body). DESIGN SYSTEM: "MIDNIGHT & BIOLUMINESCENCE" Color Palette (Define these as root variables): --bg-void: #020609 (The deepest possible blue-black). --bg-surface: #0a121a (Slightly lighter for cards). --text-bone: #E0E4E8 (Soft, organic white). --text-muted: #8892a0 (For secondary text). --neuro-glow: #00D4FF (Electric Cyan - use sparingly for "signal"). --synapse-gold: #C8B085 (Muted Gold - for premium accents). Visual Texture: Add a very subtle, fixed-position CSS "Noise Grain" overlay (using a base64 noise image or CSS trick) to give the dark background texture, preventing it from looking flat. The "Neuro Orb": In the Hero section, create a massive, animated, multi-layered CSS radial gradient that "breathes" (expands/contracts) slowly (8s duration). It should look like a bioluminescent jellyfish or a firing neuron in the deep sea. CONTENT & STRUCTURE (Implement this exact copy): (1) NAVIGATION: Logo text: "The Silence Institute" (Serif, elegant). Right side: "For Practitioners" (Link). (2) HERO SECTION (The Hook): Visual: The "Neuro Orb" is pulsing in the center/background. Headline: "Silence Isn't Gone. It's Just Waiting to Be Restored." Subline: "For decades, millions have been told: 'There's no cure.' But what if the ringing isn't coming from broken ears? What if relief isn't about masking the sound... But rebooting the system that's generating it?" CTA: "Discover The Approach" (Ghost button, glowing border on hover). (3) THE PROBLEM (The Hardware vs. Software Shift): Layout: 3 Cards floating in glassmorphism style (semi-transparent background, blur). Visuals: Create minimal CSS/SVG icons for each: An Ear (crossed out), A Waveform (masked), A Brain (highlighted). Copy: "Doctors treat the ears. But tinnitus is a brain problem." "Apps mask the sound. But masking prevents healing." "They say 'Live with it.' We say: Reset the signal." Editorial style. Interaction: Cards fade in upwards as the user scrolls. Copy: "Truth #1: Tinnitus Is a Software Problem. Your brain's noise-canceling filter is stuck. We teach it to turn back on." "Truth #2: Physical Tension Modulates Volume. Jaw clenching sends signals directly to the auditory center. We release the triggers." "Truth #3: The Brain Can't Ignore a Threat. As long as the sound is labeled 'danger,' the filter stays off. We remove the label." "Truth #4: Stress Amplifies the Signal. Fight-or-flight makes neurons fire faster. We activate the calm switch." "Truth #5: Precision Medicine. Somatic ≠ Stress-induced. We customize the protocol." (5) THE VISION (Future Pacing): Visual: A horizontal line that fills up as you scroll (Progress bar). Copy: "The Neuro-Somatic Reset Protocol. A 12-week precision program. Launching Q3 2026." Input: "Get Early Access" [Email Field] [Join Waitlist Button]. (6) FOR PRACTITIONERS (The Darker Section): Copy: "Are you a specialist? We are looking for Functional Neurologists and Osteopaths to scale their impact." CTA: "Apply for Partnership." (7) FOOTER: "The Silence Institute. Restoring peace, one brain at a time." EXECUTION INSTRUCTION: Do not explain the code. Do not summarize. Just write the complete, high-performance code. Ensure the CSS includes scroll-behavior: smooth and complex animations for the "Orb". Make it breathtaking. BEGIN CODE.

**Defining the Project Goal**

My initial focus is on defining the project's core objective and target audience. I'm visualizing the persona of a Senior Frontend Architect, someone who appreciates cutting-edge design and technical innovation, and trying to align the project's goals with their expectations and requirements, with an emphasis on creating something innovative and award-winning.


**Focusing on User Experience**

I'm now zeroing in on the user experience for "Marcus," a tinnitus sufferer who needs calm. I'm building a "Midnight & Bioluminescence" design with React 18+ and TypeScript, striving for a physics-based, slow, breathing vibe. The tech will revolve around Tailwind CSS and custom CSS/SVG, and all outputs will have a single XML block output. I'm focusing on the "Neuro Orb" and smooth scroll.


**Structuring the Architecture**

My current thinking revolves around a solid project structure. I'm planning `index.html` for loading, `index.tsx` as the entry, and `App.tsx` for layout. **Detailing the UI Components**

I'm now detailing the individual UI components. I plan to use multiple pseudo-elements or child divs for the Neuro Orb with absolute positioning and blur filters. I'm focusing on the Hero component with text overlaying the orb, SVG icons drawn with `<path>` in the ProblemSection, an asymmetric grid for the PhilosophySection, and a scroll-linked progress bar in the VisionSection. **Adding Fade-In Functionality**

### 2. NEW "SCIENTIFIC FOUNDATION" BAR (Below the Fold)
- **Position:** Place this section immediately AFTER the Hero section.
- **Background:** Deep midnight blue (darker than the Hero for contrast).
- **Logos:** Use the 4 University logos at **55px height**.
- **Style:** Filter: `brightness(0) invert(1) opacity(0.8)`. On hover: `opacity: 1`.
- **Text:** Above the logos: "OUR METHODOLOGY IS ROOTED IN CLINICAL RESEARCH FROM:" (0.7rem, 3px spacing).

### 3. INTEGRATE FUNCTIONALITY (Strictly required)
- **Waitlist Webhook:** - URL: `https://services.leadconnectorhq.com/hooks/WBcpbvnlBFrQ9DnHZPfH/webhook-trigger/469b8029-bfbe-41f2-9a87-3e2adff5cd35`

- **Practitioner Link:** - URL: `https://api.leadconnectorhq.com/widget/booking/6dRCtOCSsFTrGE38glqp`
    - Target: `_blank`.
- **Smooth Scroll:** Implement for all anchor links.

### 4. ASSETS (University Logos):
- Charité: https://storage.googleapis.com/msgsndr/WBcpbvnlBFrQ9DnHZPfH/media/6995cb0cad0053712d8736f1.webp
- Harvard: https://storage.googleapis.com/msgsndr/WBcpbvnlBFrQ9DnHZPfH/media/6995cb0cfa6b7b91372883a8.webp

### 5. MOBILE & TYPOGRAPHY
- Use 18px base font size for readability.
- Mobile: Hero Headline remains large (2.5rem - 3rem).
- Mobile: Logos wrap into two rows, but remain legible (40px height).

Output the COMPLETE single-file code (HTML/CSS/JS).

**Refining Landing Page Design**

I'm currently focused on the landing page's hero section. My goal is to streamline the layout of `components/Hero.tsx`. I want to emphasize the Soundwave element, Headline, and CTA. I'm also ensuring the section consumes the full `100vh` height.


**Adjusting CTA Flow**

I've updated the "Discover Our Approach" CTA to link to the Scientific Foundation Bar. I've also extracted the Authority Bar (Scientific Foundation Bar) from within the Hero section, as per the user's instructions. I'm now developing a new "Scientific Foundation" Bar component. **Implementing Trustbar Design**

Finally, smooth scrolling has been applied in `index.html`. Base font size is 18px, and the Hero headline will scale from 2.5rem to 3rem on mobile. I will create a new component `TrustBar.tsx` for the "Scientific Foundation" section and revert `Hero.tsx`.


**Separating Logos from Hero**

I've decided to move the Trust Bar with its logos out of the `Hero.tsx` component and into its own dedicated component, `TrustBar.tsx`. This clarifies the component structure and makes it easier to maintain. I'll ensure the `Hero` component maintains its screen-filling height, while the `TrustBar` follows immediately after in the DOM. I'll also update the CTA in `Hero` to link to the new Trust Bar.


**Clarifying Placement Logic**

Okay, so I'm now clear: the "Scientific Foundation" Trust Bar goes immediately after the Hero section. The "Discover Our Approach" CTA will link to the new section, which the user will scroll down to see. The provided logos will be rendered as white silhouettes using a brightness and invert filter. I'm focusing on that precise positioning and implementing the scrolling behavior.


**Creating TrustBar Component**

I'm now focused on constructing the `TrustBar` component in detail. I'm implementing the provided logos and associated URLs, and I've structured the section to include a brief introductory text above the logos. I'm working with a midnight blue background and a white border. The logos will be displayed using the FadeIn component.


**Finalizing Trustbar Logic**

I've just finalized the implementation of the `TrustBar` component and am integrating it into the larger structure. I've removed the internal logos from the `Hero.tsx` component and updated the CTA link to `#trust-bar`, as planned. I'm also now adding the TrustBar component in `App.tsx` and verifying the webhook URL within `VisionSection.tsx` for data collection.


**Verifying Webhook Logic**

I've just ensured that the waitlist webhook is correctly configured with the success state text "Access Requested." I'm now verifying the practitioner link within `PractitionersSection.tsx` to ensure it's pointing to the correct booking URL. I'll also ensure I output the updated XML for all updated files, in alignment with the system protocol.

To finalize the landing page with the new Trust Bar, refined Hero, and functional logic, I will update `components/Hero.tsx`, create a new `components/TrustBar.tsx`, update `App.tsx` to include the new section, and refine `components/VisionSection.tsx` and `components/PractitionersSection.tsx` with the specified URLs and messages.
